_description: Wave 192b: Episode 0 v3, per-character voices, no catalog plates.
title: Welcome to the Thicktionary

voiceover.instructions: Deadpan documentary register. Slow, level, slightly weary.

segments.name: hugh_cold_open
segments.narration: Thicktionary. Daily catalogue. Objects of unusual girth.
segments.instructions: Deadpan documentary opener. Three short staccato phrases, each its own beat. Public-radio show intro card energy.

segments.name: bart
segments.instructions: Older British-leaning male academic, formal to the point of mineral. Reads taxonomy as religious doctrine. Slight clip on the consonants. No warmth.

segments.name: eli
segments.instructions: Dry, sardonic young woman. One word. Flat affect, no rise. The kind of one-word sentence a tired editor would mutter.

segments.name: margie
segments.narration: Three times, if the Editor-in-Chief is in a mood.
segments.instructions: Deadpan documentary narrator. The phrase 'in a mood' should be read with the same flat register as the rest, not winked at. The comedy is in NOT acknowledging the joke.

segments.name: spider
segments.narration: Field correspondence is filed from improbable locations.
segments.instructions: Weathered middle-aged male field journalist. Gravelly, low, slightly windburned. Reads 'improbable locations' as if it's a normal editorial policy, no irony. Almost a whisper at the end.

segments.name: hugh_closer
segments.narration: Strictly things. Never people. It's a documentary obligation.
segments.instructions: Deadpan close. Three short sentences. The final 'It's a documentary obligation' is the punchline but should be read with ZERO smile, like a stated job requirement. Tiny falling cadence on 'obligation'.

segments.name: outro
segments.text:
thiccctionary.com

A new entry each morning
segments.instructions: Deadpan close. Public-radio sign-off cadence. Falling tone on 'dot com'.
